info.title: Agent Swarm API
info.description:
Multi-agent orchestration API for Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI. Enables task distribution, agent communication, and service discovery.

MCP tools are documented separately in [MCP.md](./MCP.md).

get.summary: List active sessions

post.summary: Create a new active session

delete.summary: Delete active session by task ID

put.summary: Update heartbeat for an active session
Update provider session ID on an active session

post.summary: Clean up stale sessions
Recover orphaned in-progress tasks for an agent
Register or re-register an agent

get.summary: List all agents
get.description: Returns agents WITHOUT the six identity-markdown blobs (`claudeMd`/`soulMd`/`identityMd`/`toolsMd`/`heartbeatMd`/`setupScript`) by default — they bloat the list by ~16 KB/agent and the overview never renders them. Pass `fields=full` to restore them, or fetch a single agent via `GET /api/agents/{id}`.

patch.description: Updates `agents.harness_provider` and upserts `swarm_config` (scope=agent, key=HARNESS_PROVIDER) so the worker's poll-loop reconciliation picks up the new provider within ~10s. No restart required. The swarm_config row is what actually drives the worker; the column mirrors the latest set value for dashboards.

patch.summary: Update an agent's runtime harness and default model
patch.description: Updates `agents.harness_provider` and upserts agent-scoped `swarm_config` rows for HARNESS_PROVIDER and MODEL_OVERRIDE. The settings apply to future provider sessions.

put.summary: Update agent name

get.summary: Fetch agent + global setup scripts for Docker entrypoint

put.summary: Update agent profile (role, description, capabilities, etc.)
Update agent last activity timestamp

get.summary: Get a single agent

put.summary: Worker self-report of credential readiness (Phase 3 boot loop)

200.description: State updated; returns the agent row.

get.summary: Single-agent credential-status snapshot for the dashboard
Bulk credential-status across all agents (powers the dashboard)

post.summary: Create a new approval request

get.summary: List approval requests with optional filters
Get approval request details

post.summary: Submit a response to an approval request

Check which of the given env var keys are currently set in process.env (presence only, no values)

200.description: Map of key -> boolean (true iff set in process.env)

post.summary: Reload global swarm_config into process.env (override=true) and re-init integrations (Slack, GitHub, Linear, AgentMail)

get.summary: Get a single config entry by ID

delete.summary: Delete a config entry by ID (including legacy reserved rows for cleanup). Global-scope deletes auto-trigger an integrations reload.

get.summary: List config entries with optional filters

put.summary: Create or update a config entry (reserved env-only keys are rejected). Global-scope writes auto-trigger an integrations reload (debounced ~250ms) so Slack/GitHub/Linear/Jira/AgentMail pick up new credentials without an explicit /api/config/reload call.

200.description: Connection result — `{ ok: true, agentName, model }` on success or `{ ok: false, error }` on any failure (missing config, Anthropic API error). Always 200 OK.

intent.description: Why you are searching. Required for agent recall-edge tracking; omit for UI browse/search calls.

post.summary: List or semantically search memories across all agents (debug/admin)

query.description: Natural-language query. If present, runs semantic search; otherwise lists by recency.

agentId.description: Filter to a single agent. Omit for all.

sourcePath.description: Substring match against sourcePath (case-insensitive). Useful for file_index memories.

get.summary: Report memory vector index health and retrieval mode

delete.summary: Delete a single memory by ID (debug/admin)

get.summary: Get a single memory by ID

schema.description: Why you are retrieving this memory. Required for agent recall-edge tracking; omit for UI browse calls.

parameters.description: Why you are retrieving this memory. Required for agent recall-edge tracking; omit for UI browse calls.
parameters.name: intent

post.summary: Submit RatingEvents to update memory usefulness posteriors

referencesSource.description: Optional external source ID this memory references. Free-form string, convention "<source>:<identifier>" (e.g. "github:owner/repo#N", "linear:KEY-N", "customer:<slug>", "slack:<channel>:<ts>", "agentmail:<thread-id>"). Pick any prefix that fits — no closed enum. When present, an edge from this memory to the external source is created/updated.

200.description: Ratings applied; per-event rejections returned in body

400.description: Validation error or explicit-self R6 spam-guard rejection

get.description: Returns pages WITHOUT the heavy `body` (the full HTML/JSON document) and `passwordHash` by default — list views never render the body. Pass `fields=full` to restore `body`. Fetch a full page via `GET /api/pages/{id}`.

List recent task sessions (root tasks + chain summary)
get.description: Each item's `root` is a slim task summary by default — the full `task` text is replaced with a bounded `taskPreview` and completion/integration blobs are dropped. Pass `fields=full` to restore the full root `AgentTask`. The full root + descendant chain are on `GET /api/sessions/{rootTaskId}`.

200.description: Recent sessions ordered by chain-wide last activity

get.summary: Get a session — root task + the entire descendant chain
List skills with optional filters
get.description: Returns skills WITHOUT the heavy `content` (full SKILL.md) by default — list views never render it. Pass `fields=full` to include `content` (e.g. for SDK consumers that read it from the list).

get.summary: Get all skills installed for an agent
Compute a stable signature over an agent's installed skills
get.description: Returns a sha256 hash over per-row mutation fields of the agent's active+enabled skill set. Workers poll this to detect skill changes cheaply without fetching the full list.

post.description: Inline source skips typecheck and is auto-saved as a scratch script only on success.

post.description: Phase 3 search is substring-only over script name and metadata.

200.description: SDK and stdlib type definition blobs

get.summary: Get a saved script by id
get.description: Dashboard read: full record including source and parsed signature.

get.summary: List versions of a saved script
get.description: Dashboard read: version history, newest first.

post.summary: Generic MCP tool proxy for the scripts SDK bridge

get.summary: Build an OAuth authorize URL. Returns JSON so the browser can navigate without losing the Bearer auth header.

get.summary: OAuth redirect target. Exchanges code -> tokens and redirects back to dashboard.

302.description: Redirect back to dashboard with oauth=success or oauth=error

404.description: No token for this MCP server

404.description: No token for this MCP server

200.description: Pending client stored. Call /authorize to start the flow.

get.summary: List MCP servers with optional filters

post.summary: Create a new MCP server

get.summary: Get MCP server by ID

put.summary: Update an MCP server

post.summary: Install MCP server for an agent

delete.summary: Uninstall MCP server for an agent

get.summary: Get all MCP servers installed for an agent
List agent logs
Dashboard summary stats
Lightweight swarm-wide counts
get.description: Single JSON object of cheap `COUNT(*)` metrics — tasks (by status), agents (by status), workflows (total + enabled), pages, active sessions, skills. Use this instead of fetching full list payloads just to count. Powers UI footers/sidebars and MCP context.

get.summary: List all registered services
List scheduled tasks
Get concurrent session context for lead awareness
Identity + setup readiness + live activity for the swarm dashboard
get.description: Single source of truth consumed by the UI home page. Identity comes from SWARM_* envs; the 7 setup milestones each emit `unverified | configured | verified`; activity counts agents alive in the last 5 min and tasks created in the last 24h; agent_fs reports whether AGENT_FS_API_URL is set.

post.summary: Live-test the harness provider's credentials
post.description: Issues a real upstream call (Anthropic /v1/models, OpenAI /v1/models, etc.) for the given provider. Updates an in-memory cache so the next GET /status reports `harness.state = 'verified'` for SWARM_VERIFY_TTL_MS (default 1h).

get.summary: List tasks with filters
get.description: Returns tasks with the full `task` text replaced by a bounded `taskPreview` and completion/integration blobs dropped by default — list views only need the preview. Pass `fields=full` to restore the full `AgentTask`. Fetch a single task in full via `GET /api/tasks/{id}`.

post.summary: Create a new task

put.summary: Update provider session ID and harness metadata for a task

post.summary: Cancel a pending or in-progress task

get.summary: Get task details with logs and attachments
get.description: Returns the full `AgentTask` row decorated with `logs` (capped by `logsLimit`) and `attachments` (pointer-based artifacts stored on the task, ordered by `created_at`).

post.summary: Update task progress text
Mark task as completed or failed (runner endpoint)

get.summary: Get paused tasks for this agent

post.summary: Pause an in-progress task
Resume a paused task
Supersede an in-progress task (terminate + spawn resume follow-up)
post.description: Marks the original task `superseded` (terminal) and creates a fresh `taskType="resume"` follow-up so a worker can pick up the work in a new provider session. Workflow-step tasks (those with `workflowRunStepId`) are carved out: the original is marked `failed` with reason `superseded_workflow_task` and no follow-up is created — the workflow engine's retry/failure policy applies.

patch.summary: Update VCS (PR/MR) info for a task

get.summary: List task templates ('To start' bucket)
Redirect to Atlassian OAuth consent screen
Handle Jira OAuth callback (resolves cloudId via accessible-resources)

500.description: Token exchange or accessible-resources fetch failed

get.summary: Jira connection status, cloudId/siteUrl, token expiry, expected webhook URL, scope/token-config flags

post.summary: Force a Jira OAuth token refresh and return the updated status payload. Useful when an agent observes an expired token via tracker-status / db-query and wants to recover without restarting the server or re-running 3LO.

200.description: Token refreshed; returns same shape as /status

409.description: Jira not connected (no refresh token stored)

500.description: Refresh failed (e.g. revoked grant, network error)

post.summary: Receive Jira webhook events (URL-token authenticated). Phase 2 stub — Phase 3 fills in dispatch.
Register a Jira dynamic webhook (admin only)

503.description: Jira not connected or JIRA_WEBHOOK_TOKEN missing

delete.summary: Delete a Jira dynamic webhook (admin only)
Fully disconnect Jira: delete all webhooks, drop tokens, clear metadata

get.summary: Redirect to Linear OAuth consent screen
Handle Linear OAuth callback
Linear connection status, token expiry, workspace info, expected webhook URL

post.summary: Force a Linear OAuth token refresh and return the updated status payload. Useful when an agent observes an expired token and wants to recover without restarting the server or re-running OAuth.

200.description: Token refreshed; returns same shape as /status

409.description: Linear not connected (no refresh token stored)

post.summary: Handle Linear webhook events (signature-verified)

delete.summary: Fully disconnect Linear: revoke OAuth grant + drop tokens

get.summary: List all users with identities, token summaries and recent events

post.summary: Create a new user (optionally with initial identity links)

get.summary: List unmapped external identities (kv-backed triage queue)

200.description: List of unmapped identities sorted by count DESC, lastSeenAt DESC

post.summary: Resolve an unmapped identity — link to an existing user or create a new one

get.summary: Get a user by ID with identities, token summaries and recent events

patch.summary: Update an existing user (profile / budget / status / email-aliases / identities)

post.summary: Mint a one-time plaintext MCP token for a user
post.description: Returns the plaintext token exactly once. Subsequent reads only expose token summaries.

200.description: Minted token plaintext, token summary and composed user

post.summary: Merge another user into this one — moves identities + email aliases, deletes source

404.description: Target or source user not found

get.summary: Paginated identity-event timeline for a user (DESC by createdAt)

post.summary: Link a new (kind, externalId) identity to this user

delete.summary: Remove a (kind, externalId) identity link from this user

post.summary: Handle GitHub webhook events
Handle GitLab webhook events
Handle AgentMail webhook events
Handle native Kapso/WhatsApp webhook events
Fire a run-scoped event signal
post.description: Emits an event onto the workflow event bus with `_runId` injected. Used by wait nodes in `event` mode with `scope: 'run'`. The body's `name` is the bus event name; `payload` is forwarded as-is plus `_runId`.

post.summary: Fire a global workflow event signal
post.description: Emits an event onto the workflow event bus. Wait-states with `scope: 'global'` may match. Run-scoped waits will NOT match this broadcast unless the payload carries a matching `workflowRunId`.

get.summary: List all workflows
get.description: Returns workflows WITHOUT the heavy `definition` (the full DAG) by default — the list view only needs a `nodeCount`, which is included. Pass `fields=full` to restore `definition` + trigger config. Fetch the full workflow via `GET /api/workflows/{id}`.

post.summary: Create a new workflow

id.description: Unique node identifier, used in 'next' and 'inputs' mappings

config.description: Executor-specific config. For agent-task: { template, outputSchema?, agentId?, tags?, priority?, dir?, vcsRepo?, model? }. For swarm-script: { scriptName, scope?, pinHash?, args?, fsMode? }. Values support {{interpolation}} from the node's inputs context. NOTE: config.outputSchema on agent-task nodes validates the AGENT's raw JSON output, while node-level outputSchema validates the EXECUTOR's return value ({taskId, taskOutput}).

next.description: Next node(s): string for simple chaining, string[] for fan-out to parallel nodes, or record for port-based routing ({pass: 'a', fail: 'b'})

inputs.description: REQUIRED for cross-node data access. Maps local names to context paths. Without this, upstream step outputs are NOT available for interpolation — only 'trigger' and 'input' are. Example: { "cityData": "generate-city" } → use {{cityData.taskOutput.field}} in config templates. For trigger data: { "pr": "trigger.pullRequest" }.

inputSchema.description: JSON Schema to validate resolved inputs before execution

outputSchema.description: JSON Schema to validate the executor's output (e.g. {taskId, taskOutput} for agent-task). Different from config.outputSchema which validates the agent's raw output.

onNodeFailure.description: Behavior when a node's task fails or is cancelled. 'fail' (default): mark the entire run as failed. 'continue': treat the failed node as completed with error output and proceed — downstream convergence nodes receive '[FAILED: reason]' and can handle partial results.

get.summary: Get a workflow by ID

put.summary: Update a workflow

inputSchema.description: JSON Schema to validate resolved inputs before execution

outputSchema.description: JSON Schema to validate the executor's output (e.g. {taskId, taskOutput} for agent-task). Different from config.outputSchema which validates the agent's raw output.

triggerSchema.description: Optional JSON-Schema describing the expected trigger payload shape. Pass an object to set/replace; pass null to clear; omit to leave unchanged. Validator subset: type, required, properties, enum, const, items. Other JSON-Schema keywords are silently ignored.

patch.summary: Patch a single node in a workflow definition

Example: { "cityData": "generate-city" } → use {{cityData.taskOutput.field}} in config templates. For trigger data: { "pr": "trigger.pullRequest" }.

inputSchema.description: JSON Schema to validate resolved inputs before execution

outputSchema.description: JSON Schema to validate the executor's output (e.g. {taskId, taskOutput} for agent-task). Different from config.outputSchema which validates the agent's raw output.

post.summary: Trigger a workflow execution

201.description: Workflow run started (or skipped if cooldown active)

post.summary: Validate a payload against the workflow's triggerSchema (no run)

200.description: Payload matches the workflow's triggerSchema (or workflow has none)

400.description: Payload failed validation; body matches the TriggerSchemaError contract

get.summary: List runs for a workflow
Get a workflow run with steps (includes retry columns)

200.description: Workflow run details with steps including retry info

Cancel a running or waiting workflow run

get.summary: List all executor types with their config and output schemas
